---
title: "TimeBack Alpha Read Packager — agent reference"
format: gfm
---

# Agent reference — Alpha Read Packager (`arpack`)

Drive `arpack.py` to assemble a **contract-valid Alpha Read course** (OneRoster v1p2 + QTI 3.0)
from QTI items + a skeleton — or `course_orchestrator.py` to build a whole course in one command. The
contract is reverse-engineered from a live production course export (1077 items) **and** cross-checked against
Ilma's `incept-timeback-plugin` skill (where we differed, she won — corrections applied below).

## The model (verified, do not deviate)
`Course (alpha_read markers + publish flags) → flat units → resource per lesson (lessonType:"alpha-read-article") → QTI assessment-test → items + stimulus`.
- Per lesson: **3–6 guiding** items (1 passage + 1 item each, carries a stimulus-ref) **+ exactly 4 quiz** items (no stimulus). The items we've sampled are 4-option MCQs; the packager accepts any of the 7 formats (it does not require MCQ-ness or a fixed option count beyond a floor of ≥2).
- Items: **all 7 r2 formats** handled through the packager (parse → validate → emit) via Ilma's RULE 1 split — JSON-safe (`choice`/`order`/single-blank `text-entry`) emit JSON; the rest (`hot-text`/`match`/`ebsr` + multi-blank `text-entry`) ride as raw `<qti-assessment-item>` XML verbatim. (`extended-text` is also routed JSON-safe but unused — no fixture emits it.) Answer key from `qti-correct-response`. **This is the packaging contract — the Alpha Read *reading* renderer displays only single-select choice + order (see Render); EBSR is auto-decomposed to two choice items at assemble.**
- **JSON-safe** items carry the **question stem** in `interaction.questionStructure.prompt` (Ilma's JSON template, create-mcq.md); options nest in `questionStructure.choices`; a stemless JSON item fail-closes. XML-only items carry their stem inside the verbatim XML and are not stem-checked.
- **App ownership (Ramish breaking change, ≥2026-06-30):** root `course.primaryApp` is the ONLY ownership setter and must be exactly `"alpha_read"`; `metadata.primaryApp` is now **inert** (mirrored for pre-cutover servers, forward-safe to drop).
- Passage HTML ⊆ `div/p/h1/h2/strong/em/br/blockquote` + MathML; **images must be S3 URLs** (no other media). Standards do **not** serialise (live course tags none).

## Run it
1. **Ingest the generator output (parse + normalize, no hand-edits):** `output_dir_ingester.from_timeback_build_output(dir)` reads its `manifest.json` + `items/*.xml` + `stimuli/*.xml` and returns normalized `{item, stimulus}` records (role inferred, format detected, raw XML for XML-only items carried byte-verbatim). Or one item: `arpack.from_qti_xml(xml)` → a struct with `identifier, title, role, format, prompt, choices, correct_id(s), rawXml, …` (`adapt_qti_item` **raises** only for `choice` items whose key isn't among the options; XML-only items are not key-checked).
2. **Ingest the skeleton:** `skeleton_adapter.from_skeleton_table("expeditions.csv")` → unit/lesson **shells with no items**.
3. **Materialize → assemble → validate → emit:** the shells must be filled with items first, so use **one command**: `python3 src/course_orchestrator.py skeleton.json out/` (generates per lesson, then assembles + runs the fail-closed validator). `python3 src/arpack.py build` needs an already-materialized skeleton (see `examples/sample_materialized.json`) and rejects bare shells.

## Validator rejects (fail-closed)
non-S3 media · MCQ <2 choices · key not among choices · **missing question stem** (`interaction.questionStructure.prompt`) ·
missing markers/publish flags · **root `primaryApp` not exactly `"alpha_read"`** (null/empty/non-string/alias→422) ·
authored `metadata.metrics` · id-coupling breaks · wrong section structure · content/prompt not well-formed XHTML after sanitize.

## Item checklist — what's enforced where
**[pack]** packager `validate()`, fail-closed · **[gen]** generation / the jsonl→QTI transform (the packager can't see it; InceptBench doesn't check it either) · **[probe]** live render in AlphaRead, unconfirmed until the test-student probe.

- **Every item:** [pack] one of the 7 types with correct RULE 1 routing; well-formed XML; root id matches the ref; every `<img>` is an https **S3** URL (no relative paths, no other media). [gen] near-neighbour distractors; Grade-3 Lexile.
- **JSON-safe items also [pack]: a non-empty question stem** (`questionStructure.prompt`). XML-only items are NOT stem-checked — the stem lives inside their verbatim XML.
- **MCQ/MSQ (`choice`):** [pack] key ⊆ options; exactly 1 key (MCQ) / ≥1 (MSQ); ≥2 options (the validator floor; live items use 4).
- **Drag-to-order (`order`):** [pack] cardinality `ordered`; key ranks every option.
- **Fill-in (`text-entry`):** [pack] ≥1 non-empty accepted answer; single-blank → JSON, multi-blank → XML with **per-blank** keys (blank N never accepts blank M's answer).
- **Hot-text / match / EBSR (XML-only):** [pack] checks **only** that the envelope is well-formed QTI, has a matching identifier, and is S3-clean — it does **not** inspect the answer key or options (RULE 1). Content correctness is therefore [gen]: hot-text's correct token IS the evidence span; match pairs are complete + unambiguous; **EBSR Part B options are verbatim from the passage and depend on Part A**. PROBED 2026-06-17: none of the three render on the reading surface (hot-text + match → blank quiz body; composite EBSR → flattens to one ~8-option question). Fix = transform to single-select choice (EBSR auto-decomposed at assemble; hot-text → single-choice verified; match → per-row single-choice). See Render.
- **`write_prompt` (Day-2 Read & Write):** 1 MCQ (name the text structure) + 1 hot-text (click the proof) — no typing. Both rows above apply; the hot-text [probe] is the load-bearing unknown.
- **Course:** [pack] root `primaryApp:"alpha_read"`; publish flags; 3–6 guiding + exactly 4 quiz per lesson; resource↔test id-coupling; no duplicate ids; `lessonType:"alpha-read-article"`.

The headline: the packager guarantees items are **well-formed and shippable**; for hot-text/match/EBSR it does **not** vouch for content correctness — that's on generation, and IB doesn't check it.

## Render (live behavior — what AlphaRead shows) — PROBED 2026-06-17
The renderer probe is **done** (each format pushed as a probe article, walked to the quiz on a test student).
**Two surfaces, two renderers — do not conflate:**
- **Reading-article surface** (`alpharead…/articles`): renders **only single-select `qti-choice-interaction`
  and `qti-order-interaction`**. `match`, `hot-text`, `text-entry` render a **blank** quiz body; a composite
  **EBSR flattens** both parts into one ~8-option question; `choice` renders **single-select even when
  `cardinality="multiple"`** (an MSQ shows but can't be answered). All push clean (HTTP 201, valid QTI, GET 200) —
  it is a renderer capability gap, not a packaging defect.
- **Assessment surface** (`alpha_read_build`, MAP-proxy): a *different*, working renderer that genuinely hosts the
  full type-set (exhaustive census: 272 mcq, 103 hot_text, 100 EBSR, 58 msq, 42 sequence, 37 match). The
  tech-enhanced formats are real content — just on this surface, not the reading app.
- **The live reading course is 1077/1077 single-select MCQ** (exhaustive census of `live_course_export`), so the
  reading renderer's set covers 100% of real reading content. Keep single-select MCQ (+ order) as the default.
- **Transform playbook for the reading surface** (each verified or render-safe): **EBSR → two linked
  single-select choice items — now done automatically at assemble time** (`_emit_items`/`_ebsr_to_choice_specs`);
  never push a composite EBSR (it flattens). hot-text → single-choice (verified renders). match → one
  single-choice MCQ **per row** (NOT one MSQ — the renderer is single-select). text-entry → single-choice MCQ
  (or test the JSON-modeled push path, untested). Escalate match/hot-text/text-entry reading-render to the Alpha
  Read team as renderer parity — not urgent (live reading is all MCQ).

## Cross-check vs Ilma's skill — corrections APPLIED
- **D3** component-resource links carry `status:"active"` + `metadata.lessonType` (else 422).
- **G2** every test + item carries `qti-outcome-declaration[SCORE]` (else score always 0).
- **G1** all stimulus/choice/**prompt** content runs through the vendored `sanitize_html.full_sanitize` (XHTML); `validate()` gates on well-formedness. *Highest-value — the #1 render bug.*
- **G3** the question **stem** is emitted into `interaction.questionStructure.prompt` (+ options under `questionStructure.choices`) — Ilma's JSON template (create-mcq.md / create-frq.md). Previously the parsed prompt was dropped on emit → items rendered as naked choices. Now fail-closed.
- **A2** root `primaryApp` is the validator's **hard gate** (exact `"alpha_read"`; null/empty/non-string/alias → 422); `metadata.primaryApp` relaxed to **inert/optional** (package valid if dropped; flagged only if present-and-wrong).
- **D4** item-ref `href` = bare `{id}.xml`; **D5** MCQ choice floor relaxed to ≥2; resource `url` → https.
- **D1/D2 (Ilma RULE 1 — IMPLEMENTED):** JSON-safe is exactly `choice` / `extended-text` / `order` / single-blank `text-entry` → emitted as JSON. `hottext` / `match` / `associate` / `inline-choice` / `gap-match` / `ebsr` / `select-point` **and multi-blank `text-entry`** → emitted as the raw-XML envelope `{"format":"xml","xml":<rawXml>}`, carried byte-verbatim (the JSON→XML converter corrupts their scoring otherwise). The invented `map_response` template is gone (Ilma uses `match_correct`). The split is decided **per item** (`_is_xml_only`); `validate()` checks XML-only items as a well-formed envelope, not as a JSON option model.

## Upload — two real targets
`arpack` is a clean offline emitter; the upload is a separate step. Two verified/known surfaces:

**Demo (verified, no real creds):** the [platform3 content-alpha](https://platform3-andymontgomery-9773s-projects.vercel.app/content/alpha/implementation/api)
`demo` tenant. `POST /dev/mint?tenantId=demo` → token; `POST /tenants/demo/alpha/content/imports/qti-package`
with `Content-Type: application/xml` + an `Idempotency-Key` header + the raw item XML body (the surface parses
it — its rule: do NOT parse QTI locally). Read back `…/items/{id}/trust` (expect `trust_status:"trusted"`) and
`…/student-view`. This is a real TimeBack Content surface (its QC trusts our generated hot-text/match/ebsr),
but a sandbox tenant — NOT the live Alpha Read app, so it does not answer the render question.

**Live Alpha Read (production):** push via **Ilma's `/timeback` skill**
([incept-timeback-plugin](https://github.com/ilmych/incept-timeback-plugin)), NOT hand-rolled — she owns auth
(Cognito body creds + token cache + 401 refresh), the push order (S3→course→units→lessons→items→tests→stimuli→
resources(trailing `/`)→links, 409=success, checkpoint each), JSON-vs-XML branching, and GET-modify-PUT.
Pre-flight: write-scoped Cognito client (via gitignored `.env`) + `POST /validate`. Push only to a
`STAN-PROBE-DELETEME…` draft; post-push, open ≥1 lesson in the student UI (200 ≠ rendered).
**Done once (2026-06-17):** items POST verbatim as `{"format":"xml","xml":<raw>}` to `…/api/assessment-items`;
test → `…/api/assessment-tests`; stimuli → `…/api/stimuli` (`{identifier,title,content}`); OneRoster course/
components/resources(`/resources/v1p2/resources/`)/links under `…/rostering/v1p2/...`. **Org gotcha:** a draft
must be created in an org the viewer belongs to — the documented example org returned "Access denied" in
AlphaBuild; using the viewer's own org rendered fine. **Automated** as `examples/push_to_timeback.py`
(idempotent · checkpointed · 409=success · draft-guarded · `--verify` read-back): e.g.
`python3 examples/push_to_timeback.py --package qti_package/ --org <viewer-org-sourcedId> --verify
[--enroll-student <user-sourcedId>]`, creds from a gitignored `.env`. `--enroll-student` adds the
term→class→enrollment so the course surfaces in the student app (use a TEST student you own; never a real
child — and the student LOGIN is a human step, not automatable). The OneRoster class needs `org` (not just
`school`) and its `terms` must be in the same org (so the tool mints a draft term in `--org`).
**Alpha Read render gotcha (the #1 student-app 404):** a resource's `vendorResourceId` must be the BARE
NUMBER `<N>` and its QTI test must live at id `article_<N>` — the student app fetches
`assessment-tests/article_<vendorResourceId>`. Using the test id (non-numeric) as `vendorResourceId` →
"article does not exist". The tool now emits `test=article_<N>`, `vendorResourceId=<N>`. **The OneRoster resource AND component-resource
sourcedIds must ALSO be `article_<N>`** (same as the test): the Alpha Read DASHBOARD derives each article's
link from them — with `-res`/`-link` ids it builds a link with no `articleId` → 404, even though the direct
`?articleId=<N>&crsid=article_<N>` URL works. So test = resource = component-resource = `article_<N>`. The
student-app URL is `https://alpharead.alpha-1edtech.ai/articles?articleId=<N>&crsid=article_<N>`.
**One article = one passage + its questions** — `push_to_timeback.py` groups items by stimulus-ref into one
article per passage; lumping unrelated passages into a single article renders as an incoherent jumble (the
sample is a 7-item *format showcase*, not one article — each item has its own passage).
**Verified 2026-06-17:** clicking the course in the dashboard renders a sample passage's reading session
(passage → guiding-question unlock flow); the **choice/order** question steps render correctly. The content is a
sample showcase, not a real coherent article, and only choice/order render in-app — match/hot-text/text-entry are
blank and composite EBSR flattens (see Render).

## Verified vs open
- **Verified (offline):** ingests the real `incept-qti-sdk` output with no hand-edits (the 4-item + 13-item all-7 fixtures); parses every item of a live course export we pulled (~1077) cleanly (no wrong keys/blanks); emits a graph whose contract-invariant fields match that export (checked by the parity tests on the course header + one round-tripped lesson); the one-command orchestrator (**stub** generator) → package passes `validate`; sanitizer + Phase-1 corrections applied; cross-checked against Ilma's skill (her corrections adopted where we differed).
- **Server-validated (demo tenant):** the QTI for all 6 sample formats (incl. raw-XML hot-text/match/ebsr) imports QC-trusted to the platform3 content-alpha `demo` tenant via `qti-package` — `trust_status:"trusted"`, `published`, student-view with answer keys hidden. Confirmed for both the bridge-generated QTI AND the production `incept-qti-sdk` package (6 items, real ELA sample), which our packager also parses clean. Real TimeBack QC, sandbox tenant — not the live Alpha Read renderer (that's the cred-gated live push).
- **Live push (verified 2026-06-17):** the sample package (6 items + 6 stimuli) pushed to live Alpha Read as a `STAN-PROBE-DELETEME` draft (all 201/409 = server-accepted, not a render claim). In **AlphaBuild** (the authoring tool, *not* the reading renderer) all 6 formats show, QTI panel **Valid**, preview shows a two-pane view. AlphaBuild-Valid ≠ renders-in-the-reading-app.
- **Student render PROBED (2026-06-17):** a test student (own profile) enrolled via term→class→enrollment; each format walked to the quiz in the live reading app. Result: **single-select choice + order render; match/hot-text/text-entry render blank; composite EBSR flattens; MSQ renders but is single-select** (see Render). hot-text→single-choice and EBSR→two-choice transforms were pushed and **confirmed to render**. Only the student **login** is non-automatable (agents must not create accounts or authenticate as students).
- **Open:** a full multi-lesson course end-to-end awaits the live skeleton + full generator.
